user_text:
The process of clerks begins when a patient registers at the hospital.

The clerk starts by recording the data of each patient.

Once the data is recorded, the patient's SSN is registered, the patient's name is registered and the patient's surname is registered. These tasks are performed simultaneously.

After these registrations are completed, the process converges, and the clerk proceeds to print the referral document.

Following the printing, the record is archived in the file system.

Finally, the registration process is completed, marking the end of the procedure.
output_text:
The process begins with the initiation of the task to print a referral document. Once the document is printed, three tasks are set to occur simultaneously. These tasks involve registering different pieces of information about a person. The first task is to register the Social Security Number (SSN). At the same time, the second task involves registering the person's name, and the third task involves registering the person's surname. 

All three tasks—registering the SSN, name, and surname—are carried out independently but concurrently. Once all three pieces of information have been successfully registered, the process moves forward to the next step, which is to register the patient. 

After the patient registration is complete, the process proceeds to archive the record. Finally, once the record has been archived, the process comes to an end.
